sections.paragraph:
ἀόκνως adv. fr. ἄοκνος (Hippocr. et al.; SIG 762, 30; PSI 621, 6 [III b.c.]; UPZ 145, 46 [164 b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 5, 238; Ath. 3, 2) in the Gr-Rom. world of pers. distinguished for eagerness to engage in public service (in add. to some of the reff. cited above, s. e.g. IPriene 17, 45; SEG XLI, 1556, 10) without hesitation προσέρχεσθαί τινι 1 Cl 33:8.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

pers. pers. = person(s)

add. add. = additional

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 94
